options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-cluster --region us-central1` writes the kubeconfig entry and sets the current context.

options.text: kubectl config use-context manually with a guessed name
options.explanation: Wrong. The context doesn't exist until get-credentials creates it.
Wrong. Authenticates the user but doesn't add the cluster to kubeconfig.

options.text: Download the kubeconfig from the console as a file
options.explanation: Wrong. get-credentials is the supported path; manual files drift.

questions.stem: You must grant a teammate permission to view all resources in a project but change nothing. Which predefined role and command?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding my-proj --member='user:t@x.com' --role='roles/viewer'` grants read-only access project-wide.
Wrong. Editor allows modifying resources, exceeding view-only.
Wrong. Owner adds IAM management; far too broad.
Wrong. Delete contradicts view-only.

questions.stem: An application on a Compute Engine VM must call Cloud Storage without embedding a key file. What is the recommended setup?

options.text: Attach a service account to the VM with the needed role and use the metadata server
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud compute instances create vm --service-account sa@proj.iam.gserviceaccount.com --scopes cloud-platform`; the SDK auto-fetches tokens from the metadata server — no key files.

options.text: Download a service account JSON key onto the VM
options.explanation: Wrong. Long-lived key files are a security risk and unnecessary on GCE.
Wrong. Ties the app to a human identity; not for workloads.
Wrong. Exposes data broadly to avoid auth.

questions.stem: You need a Cloud Storage bucket whose objects are deleted automatically 90 days after creation. What do you configure?

options.text: An object lifecycle rule with Delete after age 90
options.explanation: Correct. Write a lifecycle JSON with action Delete, condition age 90, then `gcloud storage buckets update gs://b --lifecycle-file=lifecycle.json`.

Wrong. Reinvents built-in lifecycle management.

questions.stem: Data is read frequently for 30 days, then rarely. You want automatic cost optimization by storage class. What lifecycle action fits?

options.explanation: Correct. A lifecycle rule `{action: SetStorageClass Nearline, condition: {age: 30}}` transitions objects to a cheaper class for infrequent access.

Wrong. Location choice, not access-frequency tiering.
Wrong. Keeps old versions; unrelated to tiering by access pattern.

questions.stem: A managed instance group must add VMs when CPU exceeds 60%. What do you configure?

options.text: Autoscaling on the MIG keyed to CPU utilization
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud compute instance-groups managed set-autoscaling my-mig --max-num-replicas 10 --target-cpu-utilization 0.6` scales out at 60% CPU.

questions.stem: You must update all VMs in a managed instance group to a new template gradually, keeping the service available. Which command pattern?

options.explanation: Correct. `... rolling-action start-update my-mig --version template=new-tmpl --max-unavailable 1` replaces instances incrementally with surge/unavailable controls.

options.text: Delete the MIG and recreate it
options.explanation: Wrong. Drops the service entirely.

options.text: Manually recreate each VM at once
options.explanation: Wrong. Simultaneous replacement loses availability.

options.text: Resize to zero then back up
options.explanation: Wrong. Causes an outage.

questions.stem: You need to investigate why a Cloud Run service is returning 500s. Where do you look first via CLI?

options.text: Cloud Logging — gcloud logging read for the service
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud logging read 'resource.type=cloud_run_revision AND severity>=ERROR' --limit 50` surfaces the request and container error logs.

questions.stem: An alert must fire when a VM's CPU stays above 80% for 5 minutes. What do you create in Cloud Monitoring?

options.text: An alerting policy on the CPU utilization metric with a 5-minute condition
options.explanation: Correct. Create a policy (`gcloud alpha monitoring policies create ...` or console) on compute.googleapis.com/instance/cpu/utilization above 0.8 for 5m, with a notification channel.

questions.stem: You must export all project logs to BigQuery for long-term analysis. What do you set up?

options.text: A log sink with a BigQuery dataset destination
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud logging sinks create my-sink bigquery.googleapis.com/projects/p/datasets/ds --log-filter='...'`, then grant the sink's writer identity access to the dataset.

questions.stem: Resources must be organized so policies apply to all projects in 'Engineering' but not 'Finance'. What construct do you use?

options.text: Folders in the resource hierarchy with IAM/policies set at the folder

questions.stem: Multiple service projects must share centrally-managed subnets and firewall rules owned by a networking team. Which model do you use?

options.explanation: Correct. The host project owns the VPC/subnets; service projects deploy resources into them. `gcloud compute shared-vpc enable host-proj` and associate service projects.

questions.stem: A web tier in a VPC must reach the internet for updates, but the VMs must have no external IP addresses. What do you deploy?

options.text: Cloud NAT on the subnet's region

questions.stem: Traffic to a global web app should route users to the nearest healthy backend across regions on one anycast IP. Which load balancer?

questions.stem: You must run a batch container job to completion on a schedule, with no always-on infrastructure. Which approach fits ACE-level GCP?

questions.stem: A CI pipeline outside GCP must authenticate to GCP without a stored service account key. What do you configure?

Wrong. Doesn't address authentication.

questions.stem: You need to let a service account in project A read a bucket in project B. What's the correct binding?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud storage buckets add-iam-policy-binding gs://b --member='serviceAccount:sa@projA.iam.gserviceaccount.com' --role='roles/storage.objectViewer'`. IAM is cross-project by member identity.

questions.stem: You must provision a project's infrastructure declaratively and repeatably using a GCP-native IaC service. Which do you choose?

options.text: A bash script of gcloud commands

questions.stem: You must set a default region and zone so gcloud compute commands don't require those flags each time. How?

questions.stem: You must capture and analyze VPC traffic metadata (5-tuple, bytes) to investigate a connectivity issue. What do you enable?

questions.stem: You need to SSH into a private VM that has no external IP, without setting up a bastion host. What feature do you use?

questions.stem: A scheduled task must run a script every night at 2 AM and call an HTTP endpoint, fully managed. What do you use?

questions.stem: Your GKE workloads must access Google Cloud APIs using per-workload identities instead of node service accounts. What do you enable?

questions.stem: You must allow a group of developers to deploy to Cloud Run but not manage IAM. Which role binds to the group?

questions.stem: You must view current resource quotas and request an increase for Compute CPUs in a region. Where do you go?

questions.stem: A service account is too powerful; you must let one user run jobs as it without owning its key. What do you grant?

questions.stem: A Cloud Run service must only be callable by other authenticated services, not the public internet. How do you configure it?

options.text: Scale the old revision to zero
options.explanation: Wrong. Removes the safe baseline.

questions.stem: You must apply an organization policy that disallows creation of external IP addresses on VMs across all projects. What do you use?

options.text: An Organization Policy constraint (compute.vmExternalIpAccess) at the org node
options.explanation: Correct. Set the constraint to deny external IPs; it inherits to all projects. `gcloud resource-manager org-policies set-policy ...`.
Wrong. Filters traffic; doesn't prevent IP assignment.
Wrong. Roles govern who, not which resource properties are allowed.
Wrong. Metadata only.

questions.stem: A container image must be stored in a private GCP registry that supports vulnerability scanning. Which service and command?

options.text: Artifact Registry — gcloud artifacts repositories create
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud artifacts repositories create my-repo --repository-format=docker --location=us` provides a private registry with optional vulnerability scanning.
Wrong. Public and external, not the private GCP-native choice.

options.text: A Cloud Storage bucket of tarballs
options.explanation: Wrong. Not a container registry with scanning.
Wrong. Not a registry.

questions.stem: An application key was accidentally committed to a public repo. What's the immediate IAM remediation in GCP?

options.text: Disable/delete the exposed service account key and audit its usage
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud iam service-accounts keys delete EXPOSED_KEY --iam-account=sa@...`, then review audit logs for misuse and rotate.
Wrong. Doesn't invalidate the leaked key.
Wrong. The key works from anywhere with internet; firewall doesn't revoke it.
Wrong. Unrelated to credential exposure.

questions.stem: You need a NoSQL document database for a mobile app with real-time sync and offline support, minimal ops. Which service?

options.explanation: Correct. Firestore is a serverless document database with real-time listeners and offline sync for mobile/web clients.
Wrong. Relational, no native mobile real-time sync.
Wrong. Wide-column analytical NoSQL, not mobile document sync.
Wrong. Relational global SQL, heavier than needed here.

questions.stem: You must monitor a Cloud SQL instance and be alerted when replication lag is high. What do you create?

options.text: A Cloud Monitoring alerting policy on the replication lag metric
options.explanation: Correct. Build a policy on the Cloud SQL replica lag metric with a threshold and a notification channel.
Wrong. Cost, not replication health.
Wrong. Routes logs; doesn't threshold a metric.
Wrong. Unrelated to monitoring.

questions.stem: GKE pods need a persistent volume that survives pod rescheduling and is backed by a GCP persistent disk. What do you define?

options.text: A PersistentVolumeClaim using a pd-backed StorageClass
options.explanation: Correct. A PVC with the standard/pd StorageClass dynamically provisions a Compute Engine persistent disk that reattaches when the pod reschedules.
Wrong. emptyDir is ephemeral and lost on pod deletion.
Wrong. Tied to a node; doesn't follow rescheduled pods.
Wrong. For config data, not persistent storage.

questions.stem: You must move a project from one folder to another in the resource hierarchy. What command?

options.text: gcloud beta projects move (or resource-manager) to the target folder
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud beta projects move my-proj --folder=NEW_FOLDER_ID` relocates it, changing inherited policies accordingly.

options.text: Recreate the project under the new folder
options.explanation: Wrong. Destroys resources unnecessarily; projects can be moved.
Wrong. Labels don't change hierarchy.
Wrong. Unrelated.

questions.stem: A high-throughput streaming analytics pipeline must ingest millions of events per second and store them for analytics. Which combination fits ACE-level GCP?

options.text: Pub/Sub for ingestion into BigQuery (or Dataflow → BigQuery)
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud pubsub topics create events`; subscribers or a Dataflow job land data in BigQuery for analytics at scale.
Wrong. Doesn't scale to millions of events/sec ingestion.

options.text: A single VM with a local file
options.explanation: Wrong. Not scalable or durable.
Wrong. Document store, not a high-volume analytics warehouse.

questions.stem: You need to see real-time logs from a GKE workload as they stream. Which command?

options.text: kubectl logs -f deployment/web (or gcloud logging tail)
options.explanation: Correct. `kubectl logs -f` follows pod logs live; `gcloud logging tail` streams from Cloud Logging.
Wrong. VM metadata, not workload logs.
Wrong. Reads storage objects.
Wrong. Shows metrics, not streaming log lines.

questions.stem: A bucket of regulated data must be immutable for 7 years — no deletion or overwrite, even by admins. What do you apply?

options.text: A retention policy with bucket lock
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud storage buckets update gs://b --retention-period=7y` then lock it; locked retention can't be shortened or removed, enforcing immutability.
Wrong. Governs the ACL model, not immutability duration.
Wrong. Deletes data, the opposite of retention.
Wrong. Controls read access, not retention.

questions.stem: You must deploy the same app to dev and prod with different variables, using one declarative template. What approach fits?

options.text: A parameterized IaC config (Terraform/Infra Manager) with per-environment variables
options.explanation: Correct. One template, separate variable files per environment, applied via Infrastructure Manager or Terraform — consistent and repeatable.
Wrong. Not declarative or consistent.
Wrong. Drifts over time.
Wrong. Error-prone.

questions.stem: You must list all IAM bindings currently set on a project to audit who has what. Which command?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud projects get-iam-policy my-proj --format=json` returns every member-to-role binding for review.
Wrong. Lists VMs, not IAM.

options.text: gsutil iam get on a bucket
options.explanation: Wrong. Shows one bucket's policy, not the project's.
Wrong. Cost data, not IAM bindings.

questions.stem: A global HTTPS load balancer serves a single-region backend; you add a second region for resilience. How does traffic fail over?

options.text: Add the second region's backend to the backend service with health checks
options.explanation: Correct. The global LB routes to the nearest healthy backend and shifts traffic away from a region failing its health check — add the backend and a health check.

options.text: Create a separate IP per region
options.explanation: Wrong. Defeats the single global anycast IP benefit.
Wrong. DNS failover is slower and less seamless than LB health-based routing.
Wrong. Not automatic resilience.

questions.stem: You must copy a local directory of files to a bucket efficiently, preserving the folder structure. Which command?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud storage cp -r ./data gs://my-bucket/` recursively uploads; the storage CLI parallelizes transfers.
Wrong. Copies to a VM, not a bucket.
Wrong. Copies into pods, not Cloud Storage.
Wrong. Manages expiry, not uploads.

questions.stem: Developers should access only a specific BigQuery dataset, not the whole project's data. What do you grant?

options.text: A dataset-level role (e.g., roles/bigquery.dataViewer) on that dataset
options.explanation: Correct. BigQuery supports dataset-level IAM; grant the role on the dataset rather than the project for least privilege.
Wrong. Grants control over all datasets.
Wrong. Far too broad.
Wrong. Doesn't govern dataset access.

questions.stem: You need GKE to run without managing nodes at all — Google handles node provisioning and scaling. Which mode?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud container clusters create-auto my-cluster` runs Autopilot, where Google manages nodes, scaling, and security; you pay per pod resource request.
Wrong. You manage node pools and sizing.
Wrong. You manage everything.
Wrong. Serverless containers, but not a Kubernetes cluster.

questions.stem: A new team member should be able to create projects under a specific folder. Which role at the folder scope?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud resource-manager folders add-iam-policy-binding FOLDER --member=... --role='roles/resourcemanager.projectCreator'` lets them create projects there.
Wrong. Projects don't exist yet; this is about creating them.
Wrong. Read-only can't create projects.
Wrong. Billing access isn't project-creation permission.

questions.stem: You must schedule daily snapshots of a persistent disk automatically with retention. What do you attach?

options.text: A resource (snapshot) schedule policy to the disk
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud compute resource-policies create snapshot-schedule daily --max-retention-days 14 ...` then attach it to the disk for automated, retained snapshots.
Wrong. Not scheduled or retained automatically.
Wrong. For storage objects, not disk snapshots.
Wrong. Unrelated to backups.

questions.stem: An internal microservice on Compute Engine needs a stable internal endpoint load-balanced across several backend VMs. Which load balancer?

options.text: An internal passthrough/Application Load Balancer with a private VIP
options.explanation: Correct. Internal load balancing exposes a private IP that distributes to backend VMs within the VPC, not the internet.
Wrong. Internet-facing, not internal.
Wrong. Egress to internet, not internal load balancing.
Wrong. No load balancing or resilience.

questions.stem: You must enforce that all VMs use OS Login with IAM-controlled SSH instead of project-wide SSH keys. What do you set?

options.text: Enable OS Login via metadata and grant roles/compute.osLogin
options.explanation: Correct. Set `enable-oslogin=TRUE` metadata and grant the OS Login role; SSH access is then IAM-managed and auditable, replacing static keys.

options.text: Add public keys to project metadata
options.explanation: Wrong. That's the static-key model OS Login replaces.
Wrong. Controls reachability, not key/identity management.
Wrong. Unrelated and insecure.

questions.stem: A web app on App Engine standard must serve from a custom domain over HTTPS with a managed certificate. What do you configure?

options.text: A custom domain mapping with a Google-managed SSL certificate
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud app domain-mappings create www.example.com` provisions a managed cert and serves the app over HTTPS.

options.text: A self-managed cert uploaded to a VM
options.explanation: Wrong. App Engine standard has no user VM; managed certs are the path.
Wrong. Doesn't map domains or issue certs.
Wrong. Domain mapping is the App Engine-native mechanism here.

questions.stem: You must check which APIs are currently enabled on a project before troubleshooting a failing deployment. Which command?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud services list --enabled --project my-proj` shows enabled APIs so you can spot a missing one causing failures.
Wrong. Lists projects, not their enabled APIs.
Wrong. Lists VMs.
Wrong. Cost data, not API status.

questions.stem: A Pub/Sub subscriber is falling behind and messages are piling up. You must scale processing without losing messages. What's the typical fix?

options.text: Scale out subscribers and ensure timely acks within the ack deadline
options.explanation: Correct. Add more subscriber instances (e.g., autoscale the Cloud Run/GKE consumer) and ack promptly; unacked messages redeliver, preventing loss.
Wrong. Drops pending messages.

options.text: Lower the message retention to zero
options.explanation: Wrong. Causes message loss.
Wrong. Reduces throughput, worsening backlog.

questions.stem: A budget owner wants email alerts at 50%, 90%, and 100% of monthly spend for a project. What do you create?

options.text: A budget with multiple threshold rules in Cloud Billing
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud billing budgets create --budget-amount=... --threshold-rule=percent=0.5 ...` (repeat per threshold) emails on each percentage.
Wrong. Tracks performance, not spend.
Wrong. Routes logs, not cost alerts.
Wrong. Unrelated to budget alerting.

questions.stem: A GKE app needs config values (non-secret) injected as environment variables, updatable without rebuilding the image. What do you use?

options.text: A ConfigMap referenced by the Deployment
options.explanation: Correct. Store config in a ConfigMap and reference it via envFrom/valueFrom; update the ConfigMap and roll the pods to apply — no image rebuild.
Wrong. Secrets are for sensitive data; ConfigMap fits non-secret config.
Wrong. Requires a rebuild to change.
Wrong. For storage, not env-var config.

questions.stem: You must give a contractor temporary access that automatically expires after a set date. What IAM feature do you use?

options.text: An IAM conditional role binding with an expiry condition
options.explanation: Correct. Add a condition like `request.time < timestamp('2025-12-31T00:00:00Z')` to the binding so access lapses automatically. `gcloud ... add-iam-policy-binding --condition=...`.
Wrong. Doesn't expire.

options.text: Sharing a key with a note to delete it later
options.explanation: Wrong. Manual and unreliable.
Wrong. Doesn't govern IAM access timing.

questions.stem: After enabling autoscaling, the MIG isn't scaling despite high load. What's a common configuration to verify?

options.text: The metric and target (e.g., CPU target) and min/max replica bounds
options.explanation: Correct. Verify `--target-cpu-utilization` and that `--max-num-replicas` isn't already reached; a low max or wrong metric prevents scaling.
Wrong. Storage config, unrelated to MIG scaling.
Wrong. Not a scaling factor.
Wrong. Doesn't affect autoscaler behavior.

questions.stem: A multi-tier app needs its database reachable only from the app tier, not from the internet or other subnets. How do you enforce this in the VPC?

options.text: Firewall rules using network tags/service accounts to allow only the app tier to the DB port
options.explanation: Correct. Tag app VMs and DB VMs, then `gcloud compute firewall-rules create allow-db --source-tags=app --target-tags=db --allow tcp:5432` — only tagged sources reach the DB.

options.text: A public IP on the database
options.explanation: Wrong. Exposes it to the internet.
Wrong. That's storage, not VPC segmentation.
Wrong. Unrelated to network access.

questions.stem: You must run a quick container without writing YAML, exposed publicly, to validate an image. Which Cloud Run command is fastest?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud run deploy test --image=gcr.io/proj/img --allow-unauthenticated --region=us-central1` deploys and returns a public URL in one step.
Wrong. Far more setup for a quick test.

options.text: Provision a VM and install Docker
options.explanation: Wrong. Slow for a quick validation.

options.text: Upload the image to a bucket
options.explanation: Wrong. Buckets don't run containers.

questions.stem: You must impersonate a service account for a single gcloud command without changing your active credentials. How?

options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud storage ls gs://b --impersonate-service-account=sa@proj.iam.gserviceaccount.com` runs that one command as the SA (you need the Token Creator role).

options.text: Download the SA key and activate it
options.explanation: Wrong. Key files are discouraged; impersonation needs no key.
Wrong. The requirement is a single command.
Wrong. Fragile and unnecessary.

questions.stem: A Cloud Function (2nd gen) must run in response to a file landing in a bucket. What trigger do you configure?

options.text: An Eventarc/Cloud Storage trigger on the object-finalized event
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud functions deploy fn --trigger-event-filters='type=google.cloud.storage.object.v1.finalized' --trigger-event-filters='bucket=my-bucket'` fires on new objects.
Wrong. Time-based, not object-driven.
Wrong. Requires an explicit call, not a storage event.

options.text: A Pub/Sub topic with no binding
options.explanation: Wrong. Needs the storage event wiring to fire on uploads.

questions.stem: You must let Cloud Build deploy to Cloud Run as part of CI/CD. What identity and role are involved?

options.text: Grant the Cloud Build service account roles/run.admin and act-as on the runtime SA
options.explanation: Correct. Bind `roles/run.admin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` to the Cloud Build SA so pipelines can deploy services.

options.text: Use a personal account in the pipeline
options.explanation: Wrong. Ties automation to a human; discouraged.

options.text: Make Cloud Run public to avoid auth
options.explanation: Wrong. Doesn't address deploy permissions.

options.text: Share an SA key in the repo
options.explanation: Wrong. Leaks a credential; role bindings are the correct path.

questions.stem: A bucket's objects must be served fast to global users with caching at the edge. What do you put in front of it?

options.text: Cloud CDN on a load balancer backed by the bucket
options.explanation: Correct. Enable Cloud CDN on the backend bucket of an external HTTPS LB to cache content at Google's edge: `gcloud compute backend-buckets create ... --enable-cdn`.
Wrong. Governs deletion, not edge caching.
Wrong. ACL model, not CDN.
Wrong. Buckets aren't VM-backed.

questions.stem: You must limit a namespace's total CPU and memory consumption in GKE. What Kubernetes object do you apply?

options.explanation: Correct. `kubectl apply` a ResourceQuota capping requests.cpu/limits.memory etc.; pods that would exceed the quota are rejected.
Wrong. LimitRange sets per-pod defaults/bounds, not a namespace-wide total cap.
Wrong. Governs traffic, not resource totals.
Wrong. Scales replicas; doesn't cap namespace totals.

questions.stem: You need to authenticate the gcloud CLI on a headless server (no browser). Which flow do you use?

options.text: gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser (or service account activation)
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser` prints a URL/code for another device; for automation, `gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file=...` (or Workload Identity Federation).
Wrong. Fails on a headless box.
Wrong. Not the supported auth flow.
Wrong. Unrelated to authentication.

questions.stem: You want a single pane showing latency, error rate, and traffic for a Cloud Run service. What do you use?

options.text: The built-in Cloud Run metrics/dashboards in Cloud Monitoring
options.explanation: Correct. Cloud Run emits request count, latency, and error metrics viewable in Monitoring dashboards; add alerting policies as needed.
Wrong. Network metadata, not service SLIs.
Wrong. Cost, not latency/errors.
Wrong. Storage object info, unrelated.

questions.stem: Cross-project: a Cloud Function in project A must publish to a Pub/Sub topic in project B. What binding makes this work?

options.text: Grant the function's service account roles/pubsub.publisher on the project-B topic
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud pubsub topics add-iam-policy-binding topic --member='serviceAccount:fn-sa@projA...' --role='roles/pubsub.publisher'` — IAM spans projects by member.
Wrong. Over-exposes; unnecessary.

options.text: Copy a key into project B
options.explanation: Wrong. Keys aren't needed for cross-project IAM.

options.text: Add the function SA as project-B owner
options.explanation: Wrong. Far too broad for publish-only.

questions.stem: You must give a VM more disk space without recreating it. What's the procedure for its persistent disk?

options.text: Resize the persistent disk, then grow the filesystem
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud compute disks resize my-disk --size=200GB`, then extend the filesystem inside the OS (e.g., resize2fs) — no VM recreation.
Wrong. Unnecessary; disks resize in place.
Wrong. For storage objects.
Wrong. Unrelated to disk size.

questions.stem: You must ensure a GKE Deployment keeps at least 2 pods available during a voluntary node drain/upgrade. What do you define?

options.explanation: Correct. `kubectl apply` a PDB so voluntary disruptions (drains/upgrades) never take availability below 2 pods.
Wrong. Caps resource usage, not disruption tolerance.
Wrong. Scales on load, doesn't guard drains.
Wrong. Governs traffic, not pod availability during drains.

questions.stem: You must roll a GKE cluster to a newer Kubernetes version with minimal disruption to running workloads. What command and practice apply?

options.text: gcloud container clusters upgrade with a surge/PDB-aware node upgrade
options.explanation: Correct. `gcloud container clusters upgrade my-cluster --master` then upgrade node pools; PodDisruptionBudgets and surge upgrades keep workloads available during the node rollout.
Wrong. Causes a full outage.

options.text: Upgrade all nodes simultaneously with no PDB
options.explanation: Wrong. Risks dropping availability.
Wrong. Unrelated to version upgrades.

questions.stem: You must verify whether a specific principal can perform compute.instances.delete on a project before granting more access. Which tool?

options.explanation: Correct. The Policy Troubleshooter evaluates whether a member has a permission and which binding grants or denies it, helping right-size access before changes.
Wrong. Network data, not IAM evaluation.
Wrong. Cost, not permissions.
Wrong. Shows a bucket policy, not a project-wide permission check for a principal.
